name: pH of a neutral solution
question_text: At 25 °C, what is the pH of a neutral aqueous solution?
general_feedback: In pure water at 25 °C, [H⁺] = [OH⁻] = 10⁻⁷ M, which gives pH = −log₁₀(10⁻⁷) = 7. Values below 7 are acidic; above 7 are basic.
correct_feedback: Correct — neutral water at 25 °C has pH 7.
incorrect_feedback: Incorrect — recall what [H⁺] equals when a solution is neutral.

answers.feedback: Right — [H⁺] = [OH⁻] = 10⁻⁷ M, so pH is exactly 7 at 25 °C.
pH 0 corresponds to [H⁺] = 1 M, a strongly acidic solution.
pH 10 is basic — [OH⁻] exceeds [H⁺] by a factor of 1000.
pH 14 is strongly basic, approximately 1 M OH⁻.
